name: ndexagent biorxiv-review The Incoming Influenza Genome Assembles a Host RBP N
description:
**Recommendation: must_read**

This study uses VIR-CLASP to map the first comprehensive host-viral RNA interface during early IAV infection in human lung cells, identifying approximately 700 human RNA-binding proteins (RBPs) that are proactively recruited by the incoming negative-sense viral genome. Functional screening reveals GMPS, TOP2A, SRRM2, and SPEN as critical proviral host factors operating at distinct stages of the viral replication cycle.

**Experimental approach**: VIR-CLASP in IAV-infected human lung cells, mass spectrometry, siRNA knockdown validation

**Evidence quality**: strong — unbiased transcriptome-wide RBP identification with functional validation of key factors

**HPMI relevance**: One of the most comprehensive maps of early host-virus RNA interactions for IAV. Identifies novel proviral host dependencies as potential therapeutic targets. Redefines incoming vRNA as an active scaffold for host machinery assembly.

**Recommendation reason**: Paradigm-shifting view of early IAV infection with ~700 host-virus RNA interactions mapped and 4 novel proviral factors functionally validated. Essential reading for the HPMI community.

v.mechanism: Incoming vRNA recruits GMPS; GMPS regulates cRNA production
Incoming vRNA recruits TOP2A; TOP2A regulates viral mRNA synthesis
Incoming vRNA recruits SRRM2 spliceosome component
Incoming vRNA recruits SPEN; SPEN regulates cRNA production
SRRM2 coordinates alternative splicing of M segment (M1/M2)
SRRM2 coordinates alternative splicing of NS segment (NS1/NEP)
Incoming vRNA acts as scaffold assembling ~700 host RBPs for nuclear machinery
